title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
weekTheme: Boys trust themselves; men trust Jesus Jesus calls us to be disciples What does a Jewish Rabbi from 2000 years ago on the other side of the world have to do with you? The answer? EVERYTHING. Jesus is the most important man who has ever lived. Jesus changed the world forever. Jesus divided human history in two. Jaroslav Pelikan, a professor from Yale University said this about Jesus: “Regardless of what anyone may personally think or believe about him, Jesus of Nazareth has been the dominant figure in the history of Western Culture for almost twenty centuries.” Jesus changed the way we think about God. Jesus changed the way we think about sex. Jesus changed the way we think about power. Jesus changed the way we think about money. Jesus changed the way we think about death. Jesus changed the way we think about heaven and hell. Jesus changed the way we think about justice. Jesus changed the way we think about happiness. Jesus changed everything in history, but he’s not just a dead historical figure like other religious leaders. He rose from the dead, and if you let him, he can change your life too. You see, Jesus still calls people to follow him today and join his work of redeeming and restoring the world. And you are at the right age to really begin to understand what this can mean. You see, the first disciples that Jesus called to follow him weren’t some old cronies or middle-aged men. They were teenagers. Just ordinary guys like you. They

days.title: The Encounter
days.scripture: ACTS 9:1-9 Meanwhile, Saul was uttering threats with every breath and was eager to kill the Lord’s followers. So he went to the high priest. He requested letters addressed to the synagogues in Damascus, asking for their cooperation in the arrest of any followers of the Way he found there. He wanted to bring them—both men and women—back to Jerusalem in chains. As he was approaching Damascus on this mission, a light from heaven suddenly shone down around him. He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul! Saul! Why are you persecuting me?” “Who are you, lord?” Saul asked. And the voice replied, “I am Jesus, the one you are persecuting! Now get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.” The men with Saul stood speechless, for they heard the sound of someone’s voice but saw no one! Saul picked himself up off the ground, but when he opened his eyes he was blind. So his companions led him by the hand to Damascus. He remained there blind for three days and did not eat or drink.
days.reflection: Have you ever met someone who is really stubborn? Someone who just refused to change their mind no matter what, even if they were wrong? Have you ever been really stubborn about something only to find out later that you were wrong? It’s kind of weird how we can make up our minds about something and refuse to change it. The Apostle Paul had made his mind up about Jesus and his followers. He hated them. He was absolutely convinced he was right. Look at how intensely today's reading describes his feelings about Jesus. Meanwhile, Saul was uttering threats with every breath and was eager to kill the Lord’s followers. It doesn’t seem like there is any way that Saul is going to change his mind about Jesus. What on earth do you think it would take for Saul to become a Christian with this level of resistance? A good book? A Christian friend? A near-death experience? By all human standards, it looks like Saul is never going to be a disciple of Jesus. But then something truly amazing happens. Jesus appears to Saul. He shows him his power and his love, and it literally knocks Saul to the ground. He has a paradigm shift. He realized that he was wrong about Jesus and that he needed to change his opinion about him. Christians call this kind of encounter a conversion. It’s what happens when a person meets Jesus and changes their mind about him. An encounter with Jesus changes our minds, changes our hearts, and changes our spirits. This is how Paul explained what happened in Ephesians 2:4-6. But God is so rich in mercy, and he loved us so much, that even though we were dead because of our sins, he gave us life when he raised Christ from the dead. (It is only by God’s grace that you have been saved!) For he raised us from the dead along with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms because we are united with Christ Jesus. This encounter with Jesus didn’t just happen to Paul; this has happened in every generation. Men encounter Jesus and their whole lives are changed. They go from thinking about their own lives and “the here and now” to being caught up in a giant story of love and grace and redemption and eternity. What do you think of Jesus? Are you for him or against him? Have you been converted? Are you open to an encounter with Jesus? Jesus is still in the world changing men's lives. Saul met Jesus on the Damascus road. There is a good chance you will meet Jesus along the Primal Path. Stay open, and stay curious. You never know what could happen.
questions: Do you know someone who didn’t use to believe in Jesus but does now? How are they different after their “conversion?”
What do you think is some good evidence that Jesus is real and who he said he was?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they first came to believe in Jesus. What happened? Why do they think this is the best way to live?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Then I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you will be clean. Your filth will be washed away, and you will no longer worship idols. And I will give you a new heart, and I will put a new spirit in you. I will take out your stony, stubborn heart and give you a tender, responsive heart. And I will put my Spirit in you so that you will follow my decrees and be careful to obey my regulations. Ezekiel 36:25-27 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God for a real encounter with Jesus.

days.title: What Happened To Him?
days.scripture: ACTS 9:10-31 Now there was a believer in Damascus named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, calling, “Ananias!” “Yes, Lord!” he replied. The Lord said, “Go over to Straight Street, to the house of Judas. When you get there, ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying to me right now. I have shown him a vision of a man named Ananias coming in and laying hands on him so he can see again.” “But Lord,” exclaimed Ananias, “I’ve heard many people talk about the terrible things this man has done to the believers in Jerusalem! And he is authorized by the leading priests to arrest everyone who calls upon your name.” But the Lord said, “Go, for Saul is my chosen instrument to take my message to the Gentiles and to kings, as well as to the people of Israel. And I will show him how much he must suffer for my name’s sake.” So Ananias went and found Saul. He laid his hands on him and said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on the road, has sent me so that you might regain your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.” Instantly something like scales fell from Saul’s eyes, and he regained his sight. Then he got up and was baptized. Afterward he ate some food and regained his strength. Saul stayed with the believers in Damascus for a few days. And immediately he began preaching about Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “He is indeed the Son of God!” All who heard him were amazed. “Isn’t this the same man who caused such devastation among Jesus’ followers in Jerusalem?” they asked. “And didn’t he come here to arrest them and take them in chains to the leading priests?” Saul’s preaching became more and more powerful, and the Jews in Damascus couldn’t refute his proofs that Jesus was indeed the Messiah. After a while some of the Jews plotted together to kill him. They were watching for him day and night at the city gate so they could murder him, but Saul was told about their plot. So during the night, some of the other believers lowered him in a large basket through an opening in the city wall. When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to meet with the believers, but they were all afraid of him. They did not believe he had truly become a believer! Then Barnabas brought him to the apostles and told them how Saul had seen the Lord on the way to Damascus and how the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them that Saul had preached boldly in the name of Jesus in Damascus. So Saul stayed with the apostles and went all around Jerusalem with them, preaching boldly in the name of the Lord. He debated with some Greek-speaking Jews, but they tried to murder him. When the believers heard about this, they took him down to Caesarea and sent him away to Tarsus, his hometown. The church then had peace throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria, and it became stronger as the believers lived in the fear of the Lord. And with the encouragement of the Holy Spirit, it also grew in numbers.
days.reflection: Sometimes all the talk about following Jesus can be a bit confusing. It can be a bit vague. It can come with lots of expectation but not lots of explanation. What exactly does it mean when someone has an encounter with Jesus? What exactly does Jesus want from us? The Apostle Paul probably had the same questions. After he met Jesus on the road, he was really confused. He had lost his eyesight, didn’t know any Christians, and was overwhelmed. So Jesus sent one of his disciples to go and get Paul and help him learn what it means to be a Christian. As soon as Paul is settled in and feels better, he goes wild. He starts to preach and teach the very opposite of what he used to believe. This freaks everyone out and they wonder if they can even trust him. Paul’s newfound faith gives us 3 really important clues about what happens when a man meets Jesus. 1-He is given a new relationship. Saul is my chosen instrument His life is now defined by knowing Jesus. When you meet a new friend who you really connect with, you want to hang out with them and do stuff together because you love being around them. Jesus chose Paul as his very own messenger, and Paul wanted to get to know Jesus as much as possible. 2-He is given a new purpose. Saul is my chosen instrument to take my message to the Gentiles and to kings, as well as to the people of Israel. Paul was given the calling of telling people about the very person he tried to destroy. Jesus called him to share the message about himself to the ends of the earth. For a local Jewish boy, the thought of going to the Gentiles was kind of crazy. The Jewish folks avoided the Gentiles like mad. And being sent to speak to kings - how cool was that? Paul's vision and sense of adventure grew massively because of Jesus. 3-He is given power. Saul’s preaching became more and more powerful, and the Jews in Damascus couldn’t refute his proofs that Jesus was indeed the Messiah. Paul wasn’t changed by an idea or a new way of thinking about the bible. Paul was changed by the power of God. God began to flow through Paul in a way he had never experienced before. It was a like a force of electricity moving through him, but the force was God himself. Jesus wants to know you too. He wants to be your friend. He wants a give you a vision for your life bigger than just growing up, getting a job, and having some kids. He wants to give you power to be able to share his love with others. Life with Jesus is bigger and better than you know. Hang in there, an encounter is on the way.
questions: Based on everything you have heard growing up, what do you think the purpose of being a Christian is as far as it’s been explained to you?
Do you think God's purpose for people is the same for people in our day as it was for Paul’s day? Why or why not?
Are there any questions you have about what it means to be “converted,” “saved,” or “to give your life to Jesus?” If so, what are they?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. “‘Who are you, lord?’ I asked. And the Lord replied, ‘I am Jesus, the one you are persecuting. Now get to your feet! For I have appeared to you to appoint you as my servant and witness. Tell people that you have seen me, and tell them what I will show you in the future. And I will rescue you from both your own people and the Gentiles. Yes, I am sending you to the Gentiles to open their eyes, so they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then they will receive forgiveness for their sins and be given a place among God’s people, who are set apart by faith in me.'" Acts 26:15-18 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask Jesus to give you a sense of calling and purpose. Ask him to reveal to you some of the plans and things he may have in store for you.

days.title: What’S That Worth?
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 3:7-11 I once thought these things were valuable, but now I consider them worthless because of what Christ has done. Yes, everything else is worthless when compared with the infinite value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have discarded everything else, counting it all as garbage, so that I could gain Christ and become one with him. I no longer count on my own righteousness through obeying the law; rather, I become righteous through faith in Christ. For God’s way of making us right with himself depends on faith. I want to know Christ and experience the mighty power that raised him from the dead. I want to suffer with him, sharing in his death, so that one way or another I will experience the resurrection from the dead!
days.reflection: How can you tell what something is really worth? How do you determine the true value of something? Several years back, a group of thieves broke into a store. What they did has become legend in the criminal world because it was a crime unlike any other. The thieves left undetected and what they did wasn’t discovered until hours later when pandemonium broke out in the store. As it was reported, “Instead of stealing anything, they changed the price tags of everything. Price tags were swapped. Values were changed. The cleaver pranksters took the $395 price tag off a camera and stuck on it a $5.00 box of stationery. The $5.95 sticker for a paper-back book was placed on an outboard motor. The thieves re-priced everything in the store.” When the employees showed up they never noticed until the customers came and the chaos began. Nothing was worth its true value; everything was mixed up. Some people got the deal of a lifetime, others got ripped off. Everyone was frustrated and they had to shut the whole store down to reprice everything according to what it was actually worth. In some ways, this is what Satan and the world around us have done to the value of things in our lives. Sin mixes up the prices and values of things and that’s where there is so much chaos in the world. For Paul, he needed help having his values readjusted, and this is exactly what happened when he met Jesus. Paul thought he was really good. He valued his own devotion to God. He was really dedicated to doing the right thing, following all the rules, being a part of the in-crowd, and looking down on everyone else. He thought that God would accept him and like him more than other people because of how good he was. Yet when he met Jesus, he realized God doesn’t value our best efforts and good works to try and earn forgiveness or get us to like him. Our good works are never enough. God just wants us to trust him. To trust what Jesus has done on our behalf and rejoice in that. Paul learned that what he valued was rubbish, and that what he hated before was actually priceless. As a young guy trying to figure out what to value in your life, you have to be aware of how distorted things are. Jesus wants to save you from getting ripped off by the world, so you can have the stuff that really matters. Peace, acceptance, belonging, love, purpose, adventure, and joy. Why don’t you ask Jesus to adjust your values based on your relationship with him and what he values? There is massive wealth and treasure that so many are missing out on. Don’t be one of them.
questions: What are some things that guys your age tend to value, but you can already see that they are kind of worthless?
What would you say to someone who says that “Jesus is worthless?” How would you try and show them a different perspective?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how Jesus rearranged their values so they didn’t get ripped off? What were some of the biggest changes? Ask them what advice they would have for you dealing with the distorted values of our age.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. When God our Savior revealed his kindness and love, he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit. He generously poured out the Spirit upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior. Because of his grace he made us right in his sight and gave us confidence that we will inherit eternal life. Titus 3:4-7 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to reveal to you what really matters. Ask him for help in determining the good stuff from the rubbish.

days.title: Pressing Into Passion
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 3:12 -17 I don’t mean to say that I have already achieved these things or that I have already reached perfection. But I press on to possess that perfection for which Christ Jesus first possessed me. No, dear brothers and sisters, I have not achieved it, but I focus on this one thing: Forgetting the past and looking forward to what lies ahead, I press on to reach the end of the race and receive the heavenly prize for which God, through Christ Jesus, is calling us. Let all who are spiritually mature agree on these things. If you disagree on some point, I believe God will make it plain to you. But we must hold on to the progress we have already made. Dear brothers and sisters, pattern your lives after mine, and learn from those who follow our example.
days.reflection: What do you want? I mean really want? Have you ever really thought about that? Sometimes people don’t understand what life with Jesus is really about. They think that being a Christian is about trying to be good. They think it’s about missing out on all the fun stuff others get to do. They think it’s about trying to get rid of your passion and desires and dressing in a white robe and desiring nothing. This is a massive distortion. Look at what your man C.S. Lewis wrote about this. “It would seem that Our Lord finds our desires not too strong, but too weak. We are half-hearted creatures, fooling about with drink and sex and ambition when infinite joy is offered us, like an ignorant child who wants to go on making mud pies in a slum because he cannot imagine what is meant by the offer of a holiday at the sea. We are far too easily pleased.” Following Jesus is about having a full heart and massive desires and a radical pursuit after them. It's about living for “infinite joy.” Becoming a Christian doesn’t make you passive; it makes you passionate. This is what happened when Paul met Jesus, and he describes this in the passage from today. Look at the intensity of the language he used to talk about his relationship with Christ. Press, possess, focus, forgetting, holding, progress. Sometimes guys your age jump from one thing to another as they are trying to explore and figure out what really matters to them. This is an important part of the discovery process as you grow into a man. But make sure you explore life with Jesus. Keep doing what you are doing here on the Primal Path. You may not see it in the daily details, but you are cultivating a passionate faith that will burst into flame one day. Learn to delight in the Lord and your passion will be rewarded. Look at the promise of Psalms 37:4: “Take delight in the LORD, and he will give you your heart’s desires.” Paul wants you to know that Jesus is the best thing to pursue and the one worthy of our passion. Just like people are devoted to their favorite sports teams, video games, food, and friends, you can be passionate about Jesus. And you never have to wear a white robe. ☺
questions: Take a moment and write down 3 things you can think of that you really want right now. What are they, and why do they matter to you?
Who do you know who is super passionate about something and pursuing it really hard? How does this show up in their life and impact how they live?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how Jesus became a passion for them. What choices and decisions have they made to really seek after him?

thoughts.verse: The one thing I ask of the LORD— the thing I seek most— is to live in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, delighting in the LORD’s perfections and meditating in his Temple.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to make you a more passionate man. Ask him to fill you with vision and resolve to seek him more.

days.title: Dying To Live
days.scripture: ACTS 20:22-24 “And now I am bound by the Spirit to go to Jerusalem. I don’t know what awaits me, except that the Holy Spirit tells me in city after city that jail and suffering lie ahead. But my life is worth nothing to me unless I use it for finishing the work assigned me by the Lord Jesus —the work of telling others the Good News about the wonderful grace of God."
days.reflection: Do you know what a manifesto is? It’s a weird kind of word that gets thrown around sometimes, but it’s an important word, A manifesto is kind of like a mission statement. It’s a really clear set of principles that guide what a man thinks, loves, pursues, and does. It helps him have a sense of mission for the future and to say no to things that don’t matter to him and yes to the things that do. It filters out the trivial from the important so that he has a sense of calling and purpose in his life. A man should have a manifesto. Without it, he will drift through life, chasing one small pleasure after another without getting anywhere that matters and doing anything of consequence. When Paul met Jesus, he was given a manifesto. He was given a mission and calling for what to do with the rest of his life. In today’s reading, he shares what it is. telling others the Good News about the wonderful grace of God. Paul's life was changed by meeting Jesus. What he wanted changed, who he spent time with changed, what mattered to him changed, and what he did for a living changed. Jesus filled Paul's life with a sense of wonder and adventure like he never could have imagined. The grace of God set him free from sin and shame and filled him with joy and hope. And he wanted everyone to know that. Paul's manifesto was sharing the grace of God wherever he could. He really believed that… Jesus was better Better than the momentary pleasure of sin that is fun at first but leaves you feeling guilty. Better than trying to make people like you by doing stupid things to fit in. Better than trying to be good and feeling superior to others. Better than being selfish and building everything yourself. And he believed that Jesus was worth it. Worth giving up short-term pleasure for. Worth the criticism. Worth being rejected. Worth a bit of suffering. Paul believed all this because Jesus was the relationship that Paul had been looking for all his life. He wanted someone who would know him completely and not reject him, see all his faults and still accept him. Give him a purpose that filled him with joy, and give him eternal life so he could live forever and not fear death. Needless to say, Paul was thrilled that Jesus had called him his friend. Jesus wants to do the same things for you that he did for Paul. He wants to give you meaning and purpose; he wants to give you a mission and a manifesto for your life. The only question that remain is if you want that too. Only you can figure that out. Keep exploring because life with Jesus gets better than you can comprehend right now. I hope you too will come to see that Jesus is better and Jesus is worth it because he is.
questions: If Jesus showed up in your bedroom one day and said that you could ask him any questions you wanted, can you think of a couple of things you would want to know?
What are 2 or 3 of your most valuable possession right now? Why are they meaningful to you?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share their life vision or manifesto. How did they come to know this was the right thing for them?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. I know the LORD is always with me. I will not be shaken, for he is right beside me. No wonder my heart is glad, and I rejoice. My body rests in safety. For you will not leave my soul among the dead or allow your holy one to rot in the grave. You will show me the way of life, granting me the joy of your presence and the pleasures of living with you forever. Psalms 16:8-11 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a cause worth living for. Ask him to give you a manifesto.

extras: WEEK 2 MANSCHOOL THE STILL SMALL VOICE MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: THE STILL SMALL VOICE Blaise Pascal said that most of the problems in our lives come from the fact that a man can’t sit alone in a room for an hour. Most young guys are so overstimulated from media, the news, screens, emails, and friends that they have never created space to even listen for the voice of God or figure out what they want in their hearts. This man school will seek to confront that. Get a room in your house that has as little distraction as possible, as close to zero as you can create. You may even use the laundry room or put a chair in the middle of the backyard. Tell him he has to sit in the room (or chair) with only a pen and paper, for one hour without speaking or leaving the room. He has to write down what it is he really wants right now and any questions he may have about God, life, faith, etc. To pass the challenge, he can’t leave or talk. After an hour, ask him what he learned about himself and the things he wrote down. You will want to keep this piece of paper if he will let you have it, and use it as a prayer guide for the coming days. Then take him out for his favorite meal for passing the challenge and encourage him for being so faithful in walking along The Primal Path. OPTION 2. WATCH JESUS REVOLUTION
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
